Please send DEFER + SPEC for your metadata feature (exact syntax like 'note="..."', parsing rules, error handling, and how trans_metadata should be produced/attached to the Output node) so I can implement it correctly.

type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
spec: Add optional metadata params to trans tag parsing in InternationalizationExtension.parse(). In the initial parameter-parsing while-loop (where trimmed/notrimmed and variables are parsed, currently around line 366-401), before falling through to the 'expressions'/trimmed/else branches: after reading  and checking it's not already in variables, check if . If so: (a) if , consume the assign token, then check if  literal - if yes call  to get a nodes.Const, extract  (str) and store in a new local dict  as ; if the parsed expression is NOT a  with a str value, raise . (b) if there's no assign token (), raise . (c) after storing in metadata dict,  the while loop (do NOT add to  dict, do NOT set plural_expr/num_called_num for it). This check should happen BEFORE the trimmed/notrimmed check and BEFORE the general assign-expression / bare-name-variable branches, so 'note'/'created'/'modified' are intercepted first. Any other name (not note/created/modified) continues to be treated as a normal variable exactly as today, including if it's assigned with '=' to a non-string expression - that must NOT raise a metadata error, since only recognized metadata names get special handling. Order-independence: metadata params can appear interleaved with variables/trimmed in any order, so this check must be inside the same while loop, not a separate pass. Then thread  through: pass it as a new argument to  call (currently ~line 455-462). In  signature, add a new parameter  (place it last). At the very end of , right before , do: , then , then . Nodes without metadata (empty dict) must NOT get the  attribute at all (no hasattr). This must not affect rendering output in any case - trans_metadata is purely an AST-side attribute for tooling, never referenced in the generated call/output nodes. Test cases to keep in mind: , , , , metadata with pluralize block, empty string values  allowed, unicode/special chars allowed, and non-string-literal like  or  must raise the 'must be a string literal' error, bare  with no '=' must raise the 'requires a value assignment' error. Existing behavior (variables, trimmed, pluralize, babel_extract) must be fully unaffected when no metadata params are used.
note: Tiebreak: you claimed first per timestamp, you own ext.py. I'll stand down and not edit it — implement my metadata spec above.

type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
spec: CORRECTED SPEC (my previous DEFER message may have been mangled by shell backtick expansion - please use this version instead).

Feature: optional metadata params (note, created, modified) on the trans tag.

In InternationalizationExtension.parse(), inside the parameter-parsing while loop (the loop that currently handles trimmed/notrimmed and variable assignment, roughly where you see: token = parser.stream.expect("name"), then checks for assign / trimmed / else-bare-name):

Add a new local dict before the loop: metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}

Right after confirming token.value is not already in variables (and before the existing "if parser.stream.current.type == 'assign'" branch that handles normal variables, and before the trimmed/notrimmed check), add a check: if token.value in ('note', 'created', 'modified'):
  - If parser.stream.current.type == 'assign': consume the assign token (next(parser.stream)), then parse the expression with parser.parse_expression(). If the result is a nodes.Const instance whose .value is a str, store metadata[token.value] = that string value. Otherwise (not a Const or not a str value), call parser.fail(f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string literal.", token.lineno, exc=TemplateAssertionError).
  - If parser.stream.current.type != 'assign' (no '=' follows the name): call parser.fail(f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value assignment.", token.lineno, exc=TemplateAssertionError).
  - After handling either case above, `continue` the while loop so this name is NOT added to the variables dict and does NOT affect plural_expr/num_called_num detection.

Any name other than note/created/modified continues through the existing logic unchanged (treated as a regular variable/expression, even if written as name="something" - only these three reserved names get metadata handling).

This check must live inside the existing while loop (not a separate pass) so metadata params can be interleaved with variables and trimmed/notrimmed in any order, e.g.:
  {% trans note="test", user_count, trimmed %}...{% endtrans %}

Then thread metadata through to _make_node: change the call (currently something like self._make_node(singular, plural, variables, plural_expr, bool(referenced), num_called_num and have_plural)) to pass metadata as an additional final argument.

In _make_node(), add a new parameter metadata: t.Dict[str, str] as the last parameter in the signature. At the end of the method, where it currently does "return nodes.Output([node])", change to:
  output_node = nodes.Output([node])
  if metadata:
      output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
  return output_node

Nodes without metadata (empty dict) must NOT get the trans_metadata attribute set at all - hasattr(node, 'trans_metadata') must be False in that case. This attribute is purely for AST/tooling introspection; it must never affect the generated gettext/ngettext call or the rendered template output, and must have zero effect on templates that don't use it (babel_extract, existing variables/trimmed/pluralize behavior all unaffected).

Test cases to satisfy:
- {% trans note="Context for translators" %}Hello World{% endtrans %}
- {% trans note="User count", created="2024-01-01" %}{{ count }} user{% pluralize %}{{ count }} users{% endtrans %}  (metadata + pluralize)
- {% trans user_count, note="Message with variable" %}{{ user_count }} item{% endtrans %}  (metadata + variable, either order)
- {% trans note="Trimmed message", trimmed %}  Hello  World  {% endtrans %}  (metadata + trimmed)
- note="" (empty string) must be accepted as valid metadata
- Unicode / special characters in metadata values must be accepted
- Very long metadata strings must work fine (no special handling needed, just don't truncate)
- note=some_variable (a Name expr, not a string Const) must raise: "metadata parameter 'note' must be a string literal."
- note=123 (a Const but int, not str) must also raise the same "must be a string literal" error
- bare `note` with no assignment must raise: "metadata parameter 'note' requires a value assignment."
- Multiple metadata params simultaneously (note + created + modified) must all be captured in the same dict on the same node.
note: Corrected/clean version of my spec - my earlier DEFER message got mangled by shell backtick expansion, please disregard it and use this one
